name: agent-helper-kit
description: VS Code extension for review-to-chat workflows and agent-friendly shell tools

chatParticipants.name: bringCommentsToChat
chatParticipants.description: Copy code review comment content to chat, including file path and line number

agent-helper-kit.bringToChat.enabled.description: Enable the bring-to-chat feature (chat participant, command, and review comment button).

agent-helper-kit.bringToChat.queueBefore.description: Queue selected review comments and prefill @bringCommentsToChat until you press Enter, instead of sending each Copy to Chat click immediately.

agent-helper-kit.shellTools.enabled.description: Enable shell command language model tools.

agent-helper-kit.shellTools.autoApproveP.description: Dangerous override. When enabled, shell commands that are not already decided by explicit allow/ask/deny approval rules run without risk-assessment prompting or user confirmation. Explicit approval rules still win: allow stays allowed, ask still prompts, and deny still blocks execution. Enable this only if you intentionally want unresolved model-triggered shell commands to run without human review.

agent-helper-kit.shellTools.riskAssessme.description: Chat model used to pre-assess shell-command risk after explicit approval rules and before any user confirmation. Set this to a model ID in vendor:id format, or leave it empty to disable model-based risk assessment. Prefer a fast model for optimal experience. When left empty, unresolved commands default to explicit approval unless autoApprovePotentiallyDestructiveCommands is enabled. Commands with transient environment-variable prefixes still route through this model unless a matching ask or deny rule already decides them.
Timeout in milliseconds for shell risk assessment model prompts. When reached, unresolved commands fall back to explicit user approval instead of automatic approval.

agent-helper-kit.shellTools.approvalRule.description: Override shell tool approval rules. Keys are command names like 'pwd' or regex literals like '/^git\s+status\b/i'. Values are 'allow', 'ask', or 'deny'. 'allow' runs without prompting, 'ask' always prompts, and 'deny' blocks execution outright. Deny rules take precedence over ask and allow for matching commands. When a command begins with transient environment assignments, matching ask and deny rules are preserved after stripping those prefixes, but matching allow rules are downgraded to model-based risk assessment instead of auto-running. Invalid or vulnerable regex literals are ignored.

agent-helper-kit.shellOutput.inMemoryOut.description: KiB of shell output to retain in memory before immediately spilling to a temp file. Set to 0 to disable the size-based spill threshold.

agent-helper-kit.shellOutput.memoryToFil.description: Minutes to keep shell command output in memory before spilling it to a temp file.

agent-helper-kit.shellOutput.startupPurg.description: On extension host startup, purge persisted shell command output files older than this many hours.

languageModelTools.name: run_in_shell
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run a shell command in a cwd, workspace root, or home if no workspace. Returns an id and status (shell, exitCode, terminationSignal if any SIG* sent); poll output via id after exit as needed, or include in response via full_output/last_lines/regex. Returns the id immediately for async polling when timeout is omitted, or when timeout is reached. If a running command is waiting for stdin, use send_to_shell to send exactly one answer, then call get_shell_output again. Compared with built-in run_in_terminal, pro: stable ids, deterministic status, unambiguous/filtered/streamed/full output, smaller context footprint when only success/failure is needed; con: not interactive, no persisted shell state, no PTY-backed prompts.

cwd.description: Optional working directory for the command. Relative paths resolve from workspace root (or home if no workspace). Returns an error if the directory does not exist or is inaccessible.

riskAssessment.description: Required brief pre-assessment of the command's risk. Explicitly call out possible destructive side-effects, secrets or sensitive data leakage, loss of work or data, system or account impact, and any other potentially damaging effects.

riskAssessmentContext.description: Optional list of risk-assessment context entries. Use file paths for scripts or files the command executes, and inline strings for relevant sub-actions or alias expansions that help explain what the command really does.

shell.description: Optional shell executable path or name. Uses VS Code/default shell when omitted (preferred).

columns.description: Optional terminal width in columns. Overrides any inherited COLUMNS value when provided. Increase this if the command wraps or truncates long lines too aggressively. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 240.

full_output.description: When true, include whole output in response when not timed out.

last_lines.description: Optional output trailing line count to include in response when not timed out; mutually exclusive with full_output/regex.

regex.description: Optional regex filter for output lines in response when not timed out; mutually exclusive with full_output/last_lines.

regex_flags.description: Optional regex flags for regex (for example i, m, s, u); requires regex.

timeout.description: Optional wait timeout in ms for this tool call. Omit to start async and return id immediately. Use 0 to wait until completion and return status. Command runtime is not affected by timeout, callers must kill explicitly when needed.

languageModelTools.name: send_to_shell
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Send a single line of input text to the piped stdin of a running shell command started by run_in_shell. The command field may be empty or whitespace to press Enter. The tool appends Enter automatically, so send exactly one answer at a time, then call get_shell_output to inspect the next prompt or output. This is stdin-only and does not allocate a terminal or PTY, so terminal-bound prompts such as many SSH, sudo, or full-screen interactive programs may still not work.

id.description: The shell command id returned by run_in_shell. This may be the public 8-character id shown by the tool.

command.description: The single-line input text to send to the running shell command. The tool sends the text followed by Enter. Provide an empty or whitespace string to send just Enter.

languageModelTools.name: await_shell
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Wait for a shell command id from run_in_shell and return status (shell, exitCode, terminationSignal if any SIG* sent). If timeout is reached, can be re-polled as needed. Use this after starting without timeout or after run_in_shell or await_shell returns timedOut: true.

timeout.description: Timeout in ms for this wait call; use 0 for no timeout.

languageModelTools.name: get_shell_output
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read output and status (shell, exitCode if finished, terminationSignal if any SIG* sent; isRunning if still running) for a command id from run_in_shell. For running commands, returns only new output since last read by default.

full_output.description: When true, return output from the beginning.

last_lines.description: Optional trailing line count to return; mutually exclusive with regex.

regex.description: Optional regex filter for matching lines; mutually exclusive with last_lines.

regex_flags.description: Optional regex flags for regex (for example i, m, s, u); requires regex.

languageModelTools.name: get_shell_command
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the command that was run for a specific shell command id.

languageModelTools.name: get_last_shell_command
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the most recent command executed via run_in_shell.

scripts.lint:check: yarn lint:tsc && yarn lint:eslint --max-warnings 0 && yarn lint:prettier
scripts.lint:fix: yarn lint:tsc && yarn lint:eslint --fix && yarn lint:prettier --write
scripts.vscode:prepublish: yarn lint:check && yarn test && yarn build
